svg.title: Single-electron buildup of an interference pattern (after Tonomura, 1989)
svg.desc: Four detector frames record electrons arriving one at a time as individual dots. At 10 electrons the dots look random; by 70,000 they have piled up into the bright and dark fringes of an interference pattern, even though only one electron was ever in the apparatus at a time.

cajal:figure.book: Quantum Mechanics — Foundations: The Quantum World
cajal:figure.figure-title: Single-electron buildup of an interference pattern (after Tonomura, 1989)
cajal:figure.author: Humanitarians AI Incorporated
